title: Silicone Induced Granuloma of Breast Implant Capsule
author: Eduardo Fleury
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bfn4jmgw

steps.title: we had our first case of...
steps.instruction: we had our first case of Breast Implant-Associated Anaplastic Large Cell Lymphoma (BIA-ALCL) in IBCC- Instituto Brasileiro de Controle do Câncer (IBCC) Middle of

steps.title: we had a suspected case of BIA-ALCL
steps.instruction: we had a suspected case of BIA-ALCL. The case was confirmed as Silicone Induced Granuloma of Breast Implant Capsule. Imaging findings were similar to BIA-ALCL. Middle of

steps.title: first published manuscript reviewing the similarities between BIA-ALCL and SIGBIC
steps.instruction: first published manuscript reviewing the similarities between BIA-ALCL and SIGBIC.

steps.title: We found three common findings in...
steps.instruction: We found three common findings in Breast MRI scans regarding breast implant complications. We described this novelty findings as: - Black drop signal; - Mass with hyper signal in T2** weighted sequences; - Late contrast enhancement.

steps.title: We observed the patients with these...
steps.instruction: We observed the patients with these findings had similar clinical complaints.

steps.title: HYPOTHESIS We hypothesized that these novelty...
steps.instruction: HYPOTHESIS We hypothesized that these novelty findings were related to silicone bleeding/ shedding. We also found that silicone particles, polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS) were toxic, and develop an immune response in some patients. We speculated that SIGBIC could be a more indolent manifestation od BIA-ALCL.

steps.title: TEST We submitted the patients with...
steps.instruction: TEST We submitted the patients with radiological findings of BIA-ALCL to percutaneous breast biopsy. All patients had the diagnosis of SIGBIC with silicone corpuscles in breast implant fibrous capsule, despite the integrity of the breast implants. Most of the patients had common clinical complaints.

steps.title: PUBLICATIONS We published many manuscripts regarding...
steps.instruction: PUBLICATIONS We published many manuscripts regarding SIGBIC where we described new imaging features of breast implant complications and compares our results to that published int literature regarding BIA-ALCL.

description: VALIDATION We started a study protocol at IBCC to search SIGBIC in patients submitted to BMRI scans. Patients with breast implants and SIGBIC diagnosis criteria were submitted to percutaneous breast biopsy/ capsulectomy. A prospective observational study. Approved by Plataforma Brasil, with study protocol: CAAE: 77215317.0.0000.0072. Informed consent obtained from all patients. Approved by the institutional Ethics Committee. MULTICENTRIC VALIDATION Validation of the findings in other institutions.
